reasoning: All four nail the same correct core diagnosis (exit block/boarding as the binding constraint, a balancing loop with delay plus shifting-the-burden, induced demand refilling capacity), so discrimination comes from calibration, bias self-awareness, and falsification rigor. D wins decisively because it is the only response with genuinely decomposed, well-justified confidence: high on the structural diagnosis but an explicit 70-75% on exit block specifically, paired with a clean falsifiers section (segment ED LOS vs hire dates, correlate inpatient census, check LWBS/diversion) and the richest bias toolkit (attribute substitution, the Senge staff-swap test, hard-to-vary critique, and a denominator/"out of what?" check), all mapped to numbered Meadows leverage points. C is a close second with excellent hard-to-vary epistemics, four cheap diagnostic metrics, and a concrete refutation condition, but its confidence stays qualitative. A is strong on its data-tagged Five-Whys and counterfactual test yet offers only qualitative uncertainty and slightly thinner second-order analysis. B is clear and actionable with the best induced-demand analogy and burnout/cost second-order effects, but it is the lightest on explicit falsifiers and calibration, so it ranks last in a tight field.
